title: eriogonum covilleanum
intro: Eriogonum covilleanum, also known as Coville's buckwheat or desert buckwheat, is a perennial plant species native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, as well as its ability to soothe skin irritations and wounds. The health benefits of Eriogonum covilleanum include reducing inflammation, promoting wound healing, and potentially lowering blood pressure due to its diuretic properties. Eriogonum covilleanum can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or salve, with the plant's roots and leaves being commonly used in herbal preparations.
